commits: dea312e: test(12-S): land AC-036/037/038/050a/050c security fixtures

gate_results_on_this_host.AC-036 no-listen-syscall: BLOCKER (strace missing on aarch64 dev host)
gate_results_on_this_host.AC-037 netns-deny-egress: BLOCKER (strace missing; userns enabled, unshare ok)

blockers_encountered.summary: strace not installed on this aarch64 dev host (Linux ubuntu 5.15.185-tegra). Both check-no-listen.sh and check-netns-deny-egress.sh exit code 2 (BLOCKER) and surface a clear apt-install diagnostic; agent-security.sh treats blockers as non-fatal unless STRICT=1.
blockers_encountered.remediation: scripts/bootstrap.sh now installs strace via apt-get when available. CI runner ops must re-run bootstrap (or sudo apt install strace) before the AC-036/AC-037 gates can demonstrate green. Once strace is on PATH no code change is required.
blockers_encountered.owner_recommendation: phase 12-DX or orchestrator: re-run scripts/agent-security.sh on a strace-equipped CI runner to capture the post-bootstrap PASS evidence.

blockers_encountered.summary: TS surface scanned by line-anchored regex on .ts/.tsx (comment-aware) rather than @typescript-eslint/parser or ts-morph. Adding either would expand the npm dependency tree.
blockers_encountered.remediation: Acceptable: the banned-pattern set (legacy_* / compat_v0_5* names; deprecated re-exports) is textual and bounded. Upgrade to a real AST parser is a follow-up if/when the banned-pattern surface grows beyond name prefixes.
blockers_encountered.owner_recommendation: DEFER unless a future shim pattern requires structural matching.

blockers_encountered.summary: Rust surface uses line-anchored regex (comment-skipping) over `*.rs` rather than `syn` AST parsing. Same scope/scale tradeoff as the TS scanner.
blockers_encountered.remediation: Acceptable for the current banned-pattern set; promote to a `syn`-based scanner if the banned list ever needs token-tree awareness (e.g. macro-generated identifiers).

blockers_encountered.summary: V05_VERBS in scripts/security/ast_scan.py is empty (no canonical 0.5.x verb list enumerated yet). Scanner runs as a no-op for that rule until the verb list lands.
blockers_encountered.remediation: Append 0.5.x verb names to V05_VERBS as they become known (e.g. when CHANGELOG records the 0.5.x→0.6.0 verb renames). No code change required to start enforcing.
blockers_encountered.owner_recommendation: DOCUMENT in 0.6.0 release-notes and append verbs at GA freeze.

blockers_encountered.summary: Prompt’s required-commands list shows `bash scripts/security/ast-scan-python.py`. That fails because the file is a Python script. Invoke as `python3 scripts/security/ast-scan-python.py` or via the shebang `scripts/security/ast-scan-python.py` (file is +x).
blockers_encountered.remediation: Reviewer: please use python3/shebang form. No script-level fix because doing so would mean a bash-only wrapper named `.py`, which is more confusing than the typo.
blockers_encountered.owner_recommendation: Update the prompt template for future security-fixture phases.

discipline_notes: TDD: each new linter/script landed with a positive (clean) and negative (synthetic-shim / undocumented-removal) fixture before wiring.
Net LoC: ~720 added across 27 files (scripts + fixtures + 1 example + 1 test). No production-code change.
AC-050a + AC-050c are the enforcement mechanisms for the no-0.5.x-shim policy and removal discipline; both are live on `agent-security.sh`.
next_step_for_orchestrator: promote to 0.6.0-rewrite; respawn codex reviewer for verdict. Run scripts/bootstrap.sh on the reviewer host so strace is available for AC-036/AC-037 evidence capture.
